name: Icon.FontAwesome

aliases.name: Style
aliases.type: { size : Maybe Size, fixedWidth : Bool, bordered : Bool, inverted : Bool, pull : Maybe (ShiftX ()), animation : Maybe Animation }

aliases.name: Transform
aliases.type: { scale : Maybe (Scale Float), shiftX : Maybe (ShiftX Float), shiftY : Maybe (ShiftY Float), rotate : Maybe Float }

values.name: i
values.comment:
import Icon.FontAwesome as Icon exposing (..)

    myIconStyle : Style
    myIconStyle
      = { size       = Nothing
        , fixedWidth = False
        , bordered   = False
        , inverted   = False
        , pull       = Nothing
        , animation  = Nothing
        , transform  = Nothing
        }

    myFlaskIcon : Html msg
    myFlaskIcon 
      = Icon.i [ Icon.style myIconStyle ] (Flask Regular)
      
    i_ : (Weight -> Icon) -> Html msg
    i_ icon 
      = Icon.i [ Icon.style myIconStyle ] (icon Regular)
      
    myEasyFlaskIcon : Html msg
      = i_ Flask

values.name: layers
values.comment:
Compose multiple icons into a single icon!

    layers []
    [ LayerIcon    [ transform myTransform ] (Envelope Regular)
    , LayerCounter [                       ] TopRight "42"
    ]
values.type: List (Attribute msg) -> List (Layer msg) -> Html msg

values.name: li
values.comment:
myList 
      = Icon.li 
        [              ]  -- Html.li attributes
        [              ]  -- Icon.i  attributes
        (Square Regular)  -- Icon.i  body
        [ text "Howdy" ]  -- Html.li body
values.type: List (Attribute msg) -> List (Attribute msg) -> Icon -> List (Html msg) -> Html msg

values.name: mask
values.comment:
Compose two icons, where one is subtracted from the other.

    mask []
    FacebookF         -- cutout icon (see-thru)
    (Circle Regular)  -- outer icon (opaque)
values.type: List (Attribute msg) -> Icon -> Icon -> Html msg

values.name: stylesheet
values.comment:
Include FontAwesome 5.0.6 in your Elm project.

    import Icon.FontAwesome as Icon exposing (stylesheet,Icon(..))

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view model
      = div []
        [ stylesheet
        , Icon.i [] Meh
        ]

values.name: toClassName
values.comment:
Icon.toClassName (PaintBrush Regular)
    -- "far fa-paint-brush"

    Icon.toClassName (PaintBrush Solid)
    -- "fas fa-paint-brush"

    Icon.toClassName (PaintBrush Light)
    -- "fal fa-paint-brush"

    Icon.toClassName PiedPiper
    -- "fab fa-pied-piper"

values.name: ul
values.comment: Creates an icon list! Use this to make unordered lists with your favorite icons as bullets.
values.type: List (Attribute msg) -> List (Html msg) -> Html msg

name: Icon.MaterialDesign

aliases.name: Style
aliases.type: { shade : Maybe Shade, inactive : Bool, size : Maybe Size, flipH : Bool, flipV : Bool, rotation : Maybe Rotation }

values.name: i
values.comment:
import Icon.MaterialDesign as Icon exposing (..)

    myIconStyle : Style
    myIconStyle
      = { shade    = Nothing
        , inactive = False
        , size     = Nothing
        , flipH    = False
        , flipV    = False
        , rotation = Nothing
        }

    myTacoIcon : Html msg
    myTacoIcon
      = Icon.i [ Icon.style myIconStyle ] Taco

values.name: stylesheet
values.comment:
Include Material Design 2.1.19 in your Elm project.

    import Icon.MaterialDesign as Icon exposing (stylesheet,Icon(..))

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view model
      = div []
        [ stylesheet
        , Icon.i [ Icon.style myIconStyle ] Tooth
        ]

name: Icon.OpenIconic

name: Icon.IonIcons
